quiz.title: Unit 14 Quiz 1: Parametric Equations
quiz.subtitle: Derivatives, Tangents, Arc Length, and Speed
quiz.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A curve is defined by $x = t^2$, $y = t^3$. Find $\frac{dy}{dx}$ in terms of $t$.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the curve $x = 2\sin t$, $y = 3\cos t$ at $t = \frac{\pi}{4}$.
questions.explanation: $\frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{dy/dt}{dx/dt} = \frac{-3\sin t}{2\cos t} = -\frac{3}{2}\tan t$. At $t = \pi/4$, $\tan(\pi/4)=1$, so slope $= -\frac{3}{2}$.

questions.question: For the parametric curve $x = t^2 + 1$, $y = t^3 - t$, find the points where the tangent line is horizontal.
questions.explanation: Horizontal tangent when $\frac{dy}{dt}=0$ and $\frac{dx}{dt}\neq 0$. $\frac{dy}{dt}=3t^2-1=0 \Rightarrow t=\pm\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$. Then $x=t^2+1=\frac{4}{3}$, $y=t^3-t = \pm\left(\frac{1}{3\sqrt{3}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)=\mp\frac{2}{3\sqrt{3}}$. So points are $\left(\frac{4}{3}, \pm \frac{2}{3\sqrt{3}}\right)$.

questions.question: A particle moves with position $x = t^2 - 4t$, $y = t^3 - 3t$. Find the velocity vector at $t = 2$.
Find $\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}$ for the curve $x = e^t$, $y = \ln t$ at $t = 1$.
The position of a particle is given by $x = \cos(2t)$, $y = \sin(3t)$. Find the speed of the particle at $t = \frac{\pi}{2}$.
questions.explanation: $x'(t) = -2\sin(2t)$, $y'(t) = 3\cos(3t)$. At $t = \pi/2$: $\sin(\pi)=0$, $\cos(3\pi/2)=0$, so both components are zero. Speed $= \sqrt{0^2+0^2}=0$.

questions.question: Find the length of the arc of the curve $x = t^2$, $y = t^3$ from $t = 0$ to $t = 1$.
Find an equation of the tangent line to the curve $x = t^2 - 1$, $y = t^3 + 1$ at the point corresponding to $t = 1$.
For the curve $x = 3t - t^3$, $y = t^2$, determine the points where the tangent line is vertical.
questions.explanation: Vertical tangent when $\frac{dx}{dt}=0$ and $\frac{dy}{dt}\neq0$. $\frac{dx}{dt}=3-3t^2=0 \Rightarrow t=\pm1$. For $t=1$: $x=2$, $y=1$; for $t=-1$: $x=-2$, $y=1$. $\frac{dy}{dt}=2t \neq 0$ at both. So points $(2,1)$ and $(-2,1)$.

questions.question: A curve is defined by $x = 2\cos\theta$, $y = 2\sin\theta$ for $0 \le \theta \le 2\pi$. Find the area enclosed by the curve.
questions.explanation: The curve is a circle of radius $2$ centered at the origin. Area $= \pi r^2 = \pi(2)^2 = 4\pi$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered parametric equations.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review arc length and higher derivatives.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on derivatives of parametric curves.
scoreMessages.0: Review parametric derivatives and tangent lines.
